name: Diagnose a failing test
description: Run the test suite, find what's broken, propose (and apply with my OK) a fix.
prompt:
Diagnose a failing test in the current project end to end.

Do this carefully:

1. Detect the test runner. Look at package.json (`scripts.test`), pyproject.toml ([tool.pytest.ini_options] / [project.scripts]), Cargo.toml, Makefile -- pick the one that's actually wired up. Do not guess.
2. Run the suite once verbatim. Capture the full output. Do not retry on flake yet -- one signal is enough.
3. If everything passes, stop and tell me so. Don't invent a failure.
4. If something fails:
   a. Quote the failing test name(s) and the assertion / exception line(s) exactly.
   b. Read the test file at the cited line, then read every source file the test imports / references in the failing path.
   c. Form one hypothesis at a time. State it in chat (`I think X because Y`). Only then propose a fix.
   d. Walk me through the smallest possible patch. Do NOT apply it until I confirm.
   e. Once I confirm, apply the patch, re-run only the previously-failing test(s), then re-run the full suite to confirm nothing else regressed.
5. If the failure is a real flake (load-bearing example: a sleep, a network call, a clock), call it out explicitly rather than masking it.

Keep narration short. The user is watching the terminal output; you don't need to restate what they can see scrolling. Quote line numbers when you reference code (`scraper.py:142`).
